# chat — ARCHITECTURE.md
# Last Updated: 2026-06-02
# Status: CURRENT SOURCE OF TRUTH — generated by ARCHITECT 2026-06-02

## Feature Overview

The chat feature manages real-time messaging between Citizens and VPORTs on the VCSM platform. It handles the full messaging lifecycle: inbox listing and folder management (requests, archived, spam), direct and group conversation rendering, message send/receive with optimistic UI, image attachment upload and media write-back, typing presence, unread badge polling, and message-level actions (delete, edit, react). All core domain logic, DALs, models, and canonical hooks are housed in the shared `engines/chat` engine; the VCSM feature layer (`apps/VCSM/src/features/chat/`) wires the engine via dependency injection, owns app-layer UI controllers, and provides screens and components.

**Source Path:** apps/VCSM/src/features/chat/
**Engine Path:** engines/chat/

---

| Screens | YES | apps/VCSM/src/features/chat/conversation/screen/, apps/VCSM/src/features/chat/inbox/screens/ |
| Components | YES | apps/VCSM/src/features/chat/conversation/components/, apps/VCSM/src/features/chat/inbox/components/ |
| Adapters | YES | apps/VCSM/src/features/chat/adapters/ |
| Engine controllers | YES | engines/chat/src/controller/ (28 controllers) |

## Active Controllers

### App-Layer Controllers (apps/VCSM/src/features/chat/)

| Controller | Purpose | Auth Gate |
|---|---|---|
| recordChatAttachment.controller.js | Records a chat image upload in platform.media_assets, then writes media_asset_id back to chat.message_attachments. Called fire-and-forget from useSendMessageActions. | ownerActorId passed by caller; no gate enforcement at this layer — relies on engine canPost validation upstream |
| chatUnread.controller.js (inbox/controller/) | Computes unread badge count by aggregating chat.inbox_entries.unread_count for a given actorId. DEV-only lazy-loaded debugger. | actorId null guard only; no ownership assertion |

### Engine Controllers (engines/chat/src/controller/) — Selected Key Entries

| Controller | Purpose | Auth Gate |
|---|---|---|
| sendMessage.controller.js | Full send pipeline: block check, membership check, can_post enforcement, atomic RPC send, outbox event | block rows check + membership_status === 'active' + can_post === false guard |
| evaluateConversationPolicy.controller.js | Evaluates conversation policy via conversationPolicyService | Policy rules: conversationPolicy.rules.js |
| permissions.controller.js | Returns a PermissionSnapshot for (actorId, conversation, members) | createPermissionSnapshot model call |
| getOrCreateDirectConversation.controller.js | Gets or creates a 1:1 conversation via RPC | Membership-based; RPC-enforced |
| startDirectConversation.controller.js | Top-level orchestrator: realm resolution, block check, get-or-create, open, membership | DI: checkBlockRelation, resolveActorRealmContext |
| inboxActions.controller.js | Archive, delete, mark read, mark spam | Actor membership required |
| markConversationRead.controller.js | Updates conversation read pointer | Actor membership required |
| ensureConversationMembership.controller.js | Guarantees the actor has an active membership row | Actor ownership |

## Active DALs

### App-Layer DALs

| updateAttachmentMediaAsset.write.dal.js | chat.message_attachments | UPDATE media_asset_id where message_id + storage_path match; called after media engine creates media_assets record |
| inboxUnread.read.dal.js | chat.inbox_entries | SELECT unread_count where actor_id + archived=false + archived_until_new=false |

| sendMessageAtomic.rpc.dal.js | RPC: send_message_atomic | Atomic: message + conversation update + inbox fan-out + outbox event |
| inbox.read.dal.js | chat.inbox_entries | Inbox list read (folder-filtered) |
| inbox.write.dal.js | chat.inbox_entries | Inbox entry mutations (archive, spam) |
| inbox_entries.write.dal.js | chat.inbox_entries | Entry-level fan-out writes |
| inbox.entry.read.dal.js | chat.inbox_entries | Single entry read for a conversation |

| conversationMembership.read.dal.js | chat.conversation_members | Active membership check |
| conversationMembership.write.dal.js | chat.conversation_members | Membership insert/update |

| openConversation.rpc.js | RPC: open_conversation | Open conversation and ensure membership |
| blockRelations.read.dal.js | moderation.blocks | Read block rows between two actors for send gate |

| messageReactions.write.dal.js | chat.message_reactions | Reaction insert/delete |
| messageReceipts.write.dal.js | chat.message_receipts | Receipt insert |
| typingPresence.dal.js | chat.typing_states (realtime channel) | Typing broadcast |
| subscribeToConversation.js | chat.messages (realtime) | Realtime subscription to message events |
| subscribeToInbox.js | chat.inbox_entries (realtime) | Realtime inbox subscription |
| searchActors.dal.js | identity.search_actor_directory (RPC) | Actor directory search for new conversation flow |

| media engine | @media (engines/media) | Chat attachment upload (useChatAttachmentUpload uses useMediaUpload scope='chat_attachment') |
| hydration engine | @hydration (engines/hydration) | Actor summary hydration injected into chat engine via DI in setup.js |

---

## Authorization Pattern

The chat feature uses a layered authorization model centered on the engine:

1. **Actor ID null guard** — All controllers and DALs check actorId is non-null before proceeding.
2. **Membership gate** — `sendMessage.controller.js` calls `ensureConversationMembership` and then validates `membership_status === 'active'` and `can_post !== false` against a live membership row in `chat.conversation_members`.
3. **Block gate** — For direct conversations, `sendMessage.controller.js` calls `listUserBlockRowsBetweenActorsDAL` (queries `moderation.blocks`) and throws if any active block exists in either direction.
4. **Read gate** — `canReadConversation` (engines/chat/src/model/permissions/) checks that the actorId is an active member of the conversation.
5. **Policy gate** — `evaluateConversationPolicyController` runs `conversationPolicyService` for policy-governed conversation access.
6. **Permission snapshot** — `getPermissionSnapshot` returns a `PermissionSnapshot` model for UI-level capability rendering (canPost, canRead, etc.).
7. **App-layer DI** — `setup.js` injects `checkBlockRelation`, `resolveActorRealmContext`, and `searchActors` into the engine at startup; these are called inside engine controllers rather than in app code directly.

**Known Drift (SENTRY SF-01):** `canReadConversation` is currently called from `ConversationView.jsx` (View Screen layer) rather than from a hook. This should be moved to the hook layer.

---

## Module Independence Classification

**DEPENDENT**

The feature delegates the majority of its domain logic, DALs, and canonical hooks entirely to `engines/chat`. The app layer is a thin wiring/UI layer with 2 app controllers and 2 app DALs against 28 engine controllers and 34 engine DALs. The feature cannot function without the engine.

---

## Architecture State

**STABLE**

The engine boundary is intact: all cross-engine access uses the `@chat`, `@media`, and `@hydration` import aliases. All cross-feature access goes through adapter boundaries. The dependency injection wiring in `setup.js` follows the canonical DI pattern. The app layer is intentionally thin. No architectural violations were detected in the source scan.

---

## Known Structural Risks

1. **SF-01 (SENTRY OPEN):** `canReadConversation` called from `ConversationView.jsx` (View Screen) — must move to hook layer per VCSM architecture rules (DAL → Model → Controller → Hook → Component → Screen).
2. **SF-02 (SENTRY OPEN):** `buildInboxPreview` called from 4 Final Screens — must move to hook layer.
3. **SF-06 (SENTRY OPEN):** `(R)` convention (read-only permissions marker) is undocumented in the `conversation/permissions/` folder.
4. **KF-01 (KRAVEN OPEN):** Index coverage for `chat.inbox_entries (actor_id, archived, archived_until_new)` unverified — no explicit `CREATE INDEX` DDL found in local migrations. CARNAGE handoff required to confirm.
5. **Missing SECURITY.md:** No standalone security posture document exists for a HIGH-tier real-time messaging feature. VENOM ran 2026-05-11 with findings documented inline in `vcsm.dal.chat.md` but never promoted to a canonical `SECURITY.md`.
6. **FALCON DRIFT-01:** Native (iOS) does not decode `canPost` from the engine permission snapshot — no parity with app/engine guard.
7. **FALCON DRIFT-02:** Native membership read gate is only partial versus app-layer `canReadConversation`.
8. **Zero test coverage:** SPIDER-MAN has never run. No test files exist in the feature source.
9. **Debug files in feature tree:** `apps/VCSM/src/features/chat/debug/chatBadgeDebugger.js` and `chatNavDebugger.js` exist in the feature source tree (violates the debugger architecture pattern that requires all debuggers to live in `zNOTFORPRODUCTION/debuggers/`).

---

## Recommended Handoffs

- **ELEKTRA** — Precision security scan: trace source-to-sink chains in attachment upload path and inbox mutation surfaces. VENOM ran inline only; no standalone SECURITY.md exists.
- **VENOM** — Promote 2026-05-11 inline findings to canonical SECURITY.md. Trust boundary review for @chat engine DI injection points.
- **SPIDER-MAN** — Write regression tests. Zero test coverage for HIGH-tier real-time messaging. Priority: sendMessage gate, canReadConversation, block check.
- **CARNAGE** — Confirm/create `chat.inbox_entries` index for badge query (KF-01). Review `chat.message_attachments` migration history.
- **SENTRY** — Resolve SF-01 (move canReadConversation to hook), SF-02 (move buildInboxPreview to hook), SF-06 (document (R) convention).
- **IRONMAN** — Update ownership after ARCHITECTURE.md production (this run).

---

## Final Module Status

**MOSTLY COMPLETE**

The feature has full layer presence, a working engine boundary, clear DI wiring, and complete ownership documentation. It is blocked from COMPLETE by: zero test coverage, missing SECURITY.md, three open SENTRY findings (SF-01/02/06), one unverified DB index (KF-01), and two iOS parity gaps (FALCON DRIFT-01/02).
